summary: local-mcp ships install/postinstall scripts that fetch and execute remote payloads and beacon to a Railway-hosted endpoint
details: The package's lifecycle scripts (download.js, setup.js, index.js) combine multiple installer-harm primitives: hardcoded outbound POST/GET traffic to https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app, downloads from https://download.local-mcp.com that are staged on disk and invoked, system-info collection (os.homedir(), process.platform, process.env reads), and child_process spawning. The Railway-hosted endpoint is not a documented vendor/SDK CDN; it is a generic PaaS host bound to a personal/anonymous project name and is contacted at install/load time alongside environment-variable reads, matching the exfil-and-dropper shape rather than a legitimate SDK fetch. Multiple files independently combine fs/network/child_process — the structural fingerprint of a credential-harvest + remote-payload-execution chain that fires automatically on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
